result.text:
If Beltran doesn’t want the job -> Yadier Molina should be offered a blank check to become the #Mets next manager.

Molina is a savant when it comes to Baseball I.Q.

Veterans will respect him, he’ll be great for our younger players, and he knows how to get the best out of pitchers.

Molina’s experience & the fact he spends his time coaching in the D.R. winter leagues makes him the ideal candidate.

We need someone who has the patience & temperament to work with younger players & facilitate their growth as players.

Molina made over $160 million during his career -> you don’t spend time coaching in the winter leagues unless you love baseball & enjoy coaching youngsters who are still developing.

#LGM   #MetsTwitter

So I have a theory why Francisco Lindor has been struggling defensively. 
So in march if you remember it was mets vs pirates last game of the series lindor got sent home and got tagged out and the catcher hit him in the face and he hit his head hard https://t.co/qZaKoS68uC

result.text:
🚨FRANCISCO LINDOR THEORY🚨

So I have a theory why Francisco Lindor has been struggling defensively. 
So in march if you remember it was mets vs pirates last game of the series lindor got sent home and got tagged out and the catcher hit him in the face and he hit his head hard on the ground. (ill play the video in the bottom
I believe he has a concussion because as a far as i know he hasn’t had a concussion test and ever since that has happened he keeps making fielding errors that he never does (simple errors, forgetting how many outs they’re in the first inning)

A good comparison is anthony rizzo back in may 2023 he had a collison with tatis and hes been known as a great defender but after that he wasnt good defensively and they did a concussion test on him in september he had post concussion syndrome

So I believe lindor might have a concussion so thats why he is making these simple fielding errors but let me know what you think if its a reach or not

result.text:
#Mets 7th RD.selection, Aidan Keenan is a pitcher who could have a better professional career than his collegiate career.

Keenan fits the blueprint of recently drafted RP that #Mets transformed into prospects with legit impact SP potential.

4-seam FB sits mid to high 90s -> plus 19” of IVB 

He flashed a Cutter that was up to 92 MPH at the combine

His Slider sits mid 80s, tops out at 88 -> sweeping action

3 plus tools #Mets Pitching Lab will be focused on improving his command -> potentially turning them into 3 plus pitches.

Keenan has to improve command of all his pitches & his stamina if he has any chance of sticking as a SP.
 
Those two hurdles might mean Keenan requires more development time than other college pitchers.

If he can harness his electric stuff and improve his ability to hold his top-end velocity at higher pitch counts, he could be a fast-mover like other recent draft picks.

 Good news for Keenan -> he was drafted in the 7th Round, which is the #Mets lucky round.

Recent notable 7th RD studs -> drafted by the #Mets:

2025 -> Cam Tilly 

2024 -> Will Watson 

2022 -> Jonah Tong 

The #Mets Pitching Lab combined with extraordinary hard by the prospects -> has proven to be a formula for success.

Nolan McLean
Zach Thornton
Christian Scott

Are the #Mets best SP this season -> all 3 products of the formula mentioned above.

Jonathan Santucci 
Jack Wenninger 
Will Watson 
Cam Tilly

Are the next wave, flashing big time potential as SP, with a marginal amount of growing pains that they’re working thru.

Bad news for Keenan -> these 3 SP have set a very bar high & we are used to our 7th RD selections exceeding expectations.

I like Keenan’s chances of joining the others as a potential impact SP.

#LGM  #MetsTwitter 
 https://t.co/W6BNFaGSaJ
